description: This figure presents thermodynamic calculation results showing the relationship between the logarithm of the equilibrium solubility product (log K_MnS), the actual concentration product (log Q_MnS), and the solidification fraction (z_s) for Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels. The plot displays three sets of curves corresponding to samples 1# (1% Al, black), 2# (3% Al, blue), and 3# (5% Al, red). Solid lines represent log K_MnS calculated from thermodynamic equilibrium, while dashed lines represent log Q_MnS derived from the Clyne-Kurz microsegregation model. The intersection points indicate the solidification fractions where MnS inclusions begin to precipitate: 0.77 for 1#, 0.81 for 2#, and 0.85 for 3#. As aluminum content increases, the equilibrium curves shift downward while the concentration product curves shift upward, resulting in delayed precipitation of MnS inclusions at progressively higher solidification fractions. This demonstrates that increasing Al content alters the solidification path and postpones MnS formation to later stages of solidification.
